case_name: 243Rd Report On Section 498A Ipc
principle: The power of arrest should be sparingly drawn out and wielded only if necessary.

case_name: 63, Moons Technologies Limited vs Union Of India & 10 Others
court: High Court of Judicature at Bombay
principle: The mechanism of paired contracts adopted at the NSEL exchange between 2009 and until the collapse of operations in 2013 was very similar to the abuse referred to in Statement of Objects and Reasons to the Amendment Act.
held: By resort to such a modus operandi the transactions at the NSEL Exchange no longer remained any genuine spot delivery transactions or ready delivery transactions or even forward transactions of one day's duration, in sales and purchases of commodities.
embed_text: The mechanism of paired contracts adopted at the NSEL exchange between 2009 and until the collapse of operations in 2013 was very similar to the abuse referred to in Statement of Objects and Reasons to the Amendment Act. ["forward contracts", "spot delivery", "ready delivery", "commodities"] ["Forward Contracts (Regulation) Act, 1952"]

case_name: 63, Moons Technologies Limited vs Union Of India & 10 Others

principle: Transactions that assume the character of pure and simple financing transactions are not genuine spot delivery or ready delivery transactions.
held: These contracts were invariably at predetermined prices and the long terms sell contract, was always at a profit, the difference effectively being the cost of lending.

case_name: Aarti vs Narinder Kumar
principle: In matrimonial matters, the Courts have to take into consideration the economic soundness of both parties, social strata of spouses and their behavioural pattern.
held: In matrimonial matters, wherever Courts are called upon to consider the plea of transfer, the Courts have to take into consideration the economic soundness of both the parties, the social strata of the spouses and their behavioural pattern, their standard of life prior to the marriage and subsequent thereto and the circumstances of both the parties in eking out their livelihood and under whose protective umbrella they are seeking their sustenance to life.

principle: The Courts should consider the family condition of the wife, custody of minor child, economic condition of the wife and her physical health.

case_name: Chakradharamahanthi Vekata Maikya vs Chakradharamahanthi Vekata Ramana
principle: Unduly long delay in disposal of cases can lead to blatant violation of the Rule of law and adverse impact on access to justice.
held: A person's access to justice is a guaranteed fundamental right under the Constitution and particularly Article 21. Denial of the right undermines public confidence in the justice delivery system and incentivises people to look for shot cuts and other fora where they feel that injustice will be done quicker.
cited_cases: ["United Nations Development Programme, Access to Justice-Practice Note (2004)"]
embed_text: Unduly long delay in disposal of cases can lead to blatant violation of the Rule of law and adverse impact on access to justice. A person's access to justice is a guaranteed fundamental right under the Constitution and particularly Article 21. Denial of the right undermines public confidence in the justice delivery system and incentivises people to look for shot cuts and other fora where they feel that injustice will be done quicker. ["access to justice", "Rule of law", "delay", "violation"] ["Constitution of India \u2014 Article 21"]

case_name: Chakradharamahanthi Vekata Maikya vs Chakradharamahanthi Vekata Ramana
principle: Access to justice is not just about improving an individual's access to courts, but also ensuring that legal and judicial outcomes are just and inequitable.
held: Access to justice is, therefore, much more than improving an individual's access to courts, or guaranteeing representation. It must be defined in terms of ensuring that legal and judicial outcomes are just and inequitable [see United Nations Development Programme, Access to Justice-Practice Note (2004)]
cited_cases: ["United Nations Development Programme, Access to Justice-Practice Note (2004)"]

principle: The right of access to an independent judiciary is vital for the Rule of law.

principle: Presumption under Section 139 NI Act in favour of holder is rebuttable on preponderance of probabilities

principle: The cardinal principle for exercise of power under section 24 of the Code of Civil Procedure is that the ends of justice should demand the transfer of the suit, appeal or other proceeding.
